figures.caption: Figure 2. Overview of Structured-Attribute Contrastive Learning. (a) Positive prompts are generated via Random Drop and Shuffle to enforce permutation invariance. Hard negatives are synthesized via Attribute Replacement, creating counterfactual prompts for fine-grained discrimination. (b) & (c) Unified Learning: The model optimizes a unified contrastive objective, which aligns representations at the (b) global image level for classification and adapts to the (c) local region level (via RoI features) for detection.

Table 2. Comparison with existing methods on DOTA-v2.0 (HBB) and DIOR-H. SLIP-RS denotes the baseline trained without attribute supervision, while SLIP-RS* refers to the model trained with our SACL strategy incorporating fine-grained attribute annotations. The base detectors of LAE-DINO and OpenRSD are DINO and RTMDet, respectively.

Table 3. Quantitative results of atomic fine-grained attribute recognition on the constructed benchmark. ‘P’ for Purpose, ‘U’ for Usage, ‘EP’ for Engine Position, ‘WC’ for Wing Configuration, ‘NE’ for Number of Engine, ‘PT’ for Propulsion Type, ‘S’ for Subcategory.
Figure 3. Visualizations of SLIP-RS for attribute-guided finegrained detection. SLIP-RS not only identifies fine-grained categories but also enables recognition based on specific attributes.

Figure 4. Visualization of detection results using SLIP-RS. The figure showcases the model’s ability to localize targets based on compositional attributes and identify fine-grained categories. Note that the samples in (d), (e), and (j) are generated by Nano Banana Pro.
Figure 5. Demo examples from the Seed Attribute Classification Dataset.
